articles.title: Punjab Govt Likely to Announce Early Summer Vacations Amid Heatwave Concerns - TechJuice
articles.full_text:
The Punjab government is considering announcing summer vacations for schools and colleges earlier than usual due to rising temperatures and increasing heatwave concerns across the province.
Punjab Education Minister Rana Sikandar Hayat said the government is reviewing a proposal to start summer vacations from May 22 and reopen educational institutions on August 2.
Speaking to media, the minister said a final decision regarding the proposed schedule will be taken on May 11 after consultations with relevant departments and a review of the weather situation.
He added that authorities are also discussing the possibility of organizing summer camps during the vacation period.
The development comes after the Punjab Disaster Management Authority issued a weather advisory warning of above-normal temperatures and rainfall during May, June, and July.
According to the advisory, plain areas of Punjab are expected to face an increased risk of heatwave conditions during May because of higher-than-normal temperatures.
Last year, the Punjab government initially announced summer vacations from May 28 to August 14 before later extending the holidays until August 31 due to extreme weather conditions.

articles.title: Punjab Govt May Announce Early Summer Vacations Due to Heatwave - newztodays.com
articles.full_text:
The Punjab government is considering announcing early summer vacations for schools and colleges in response to rising temperatures and increasing heatwave concerns across the province.
Punjab Education Minister Rana Sikandar Hayat stated that the government is reviewing a proposal to commence summer holidays from May 22, with institutions expected to reopen on August 2. He mentioned that a final decision will be made on May 11 after consultations with relevant departments and a detailed review of the weather situation.
In addition to the adjusted vacation schedule, authorities are exploring the possibility of organizing summer camps during the break period to provide students with structured activities while schools remain closed.
This development follows a recent weather advisory issued by the Punjab Disaster Management Authority, which warned of above-normal temperatures and increased rainfall over May, June, and July. The advisory highlights that plain areas of Punjab are at higher risk of heatwave conditions in May due to elevated temperatures.
Last year, the Punjab government initially scheduled summer vacations from May 28 to August 14 but extended the holidays to August 31 owing to extreme weather conditions. The government’s proactive approach aims to safeguard students and staff from the adverse effects of the intense heat.
The potential early vacation announcement aligns with ongoing efforts to ensure public safety and minimize disruptions caused by extreme weather events. Educators and parents are awaiting the official confirmation ahead of the summer break.
